Transit: Tre Cime di Lavaredo hike i
Note: Why: To hike Tre Cime, you must access the trailhead at Rifugio Auronzo via a toll road (approx. €30 per car). The parking lot at the top is limited and often fills up completely by 8:00 AM or 9:00 AM, after which the toll road is closed to private cars. If you do not arrive early enough to drive up, you must take the public shuttle bus (Shuttle 444 from Dobbiaco/Toblach). Advanced online booking for this shuttle is mandatory; you cannot buy tickets on board.

Food: Once at Lago di Braies, rent a rowboat and/or walk the loop trail around the lake (3.7 km/2.3 miles). A visit to Lago di Braies lasts about 4 hours.
Note: Why: To manage overtourism, access to the lake is strictly regulated. During high-demand periods (typically July 10 to September 10, but restrictions and high crowds often extend through mid-to-late September), the road leading to the lake is closed to private vehicles from 9:30 AM to 4:00 PM. To drive there during these hours, you must pre-book a parking space online (e.g., at Parking P4). Alternatively, you must pre-book a shuttle bus ticket (Line 442 or 439) in advance. Without a pre-booked reservation, you will not be allowed to drive to the lake during peak hours.
After lunch, continue the drive south on SS51. Along this drive, you can stop off at the viewpoint of Tre Cime (called Vista Panoramica Tre Cime di Lavaredo) for a view of the three peaks.
What needs booking: Cable Car tickets or Transit planning.

Why: The road leading up to Seiser Alm is closed to private traffic from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM. You can only drive up during these hours if you are staying at a hotel located on the Alpe di Siusi and have a special driving permit. Otherwise, you must take the Seiser Alm Cableway (Seiser Alm Bahn) or public bus. While you can buy cable car tickets on-site, booking them online in advance is highly recommended to skip the ticket office lines.

Transit: Drive the Sella Pass and Passo Pordoi. Ride the cable car from Passo Pordoi for panoramic views of the Dolomites. From here, you have the option to add on the hike to Piz Boé (5.3 km/3.3 miles, 2 to 4 hours).
